vitals.entry_fee: €70 (2026); €10 optional insurance supplement for riders without a cycling-federation license
vitals.cutoff_time: Finish closes at 16:00 — an 8-hour window for the 144/122 km routes (08:00 start), 7h45 for the 78 km route (08:15 start)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 67.

course_description.character: A noncompetitive, mass-start paved-road sportive out of Camprodon in the Catalan Pyrenees, part of the three-event Gran Fondo 360 circuit. The long route (144 km, 3,600 m+) runs through the Vall del Bac and the villages of Oix and Beget before the signature climb to Vallter 2000 from Setcases; the 122 km medium route covers the same core climbs (Coll de Capsacosta, Coll de Pera, Santigosa, Coll de Rocabruna) but skips Vallter, and a 78 km short route covers just Pera and Rocabruna. Roads stay open to ordinary traffic throughout — this is explicitly a supported ride with a common start/finish window, not a closed-course race, and the organizer states no competitive time lists will be produced.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Vallter 2000 climb from Setcases on the long route only: roughly 12 km at 7.18% average (14% max, ~900m gain) — the hardest single climb on course, and the dividing line between the 144 km and 122 km routes.
suffering_zones: Coll de Capsacosta: early climb shared by the two longer routes, first major test of the day.
Coll de Pera and Coll de Rocabruna: shared by all three route lengths, the terrain backbone of the event.
Santigosa: mid-route climb on the 144 km and 122 km routes, stacking fatigue before the route's hardest terrain.
Vallter 2000 (from Setcases): ~12 km at 7.18%, up to 14%, exclusive to the long route and the last major effort of the day.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Camprodon offers village accommodations; nearby areas in Pyrenees.
logistics.transport: Start/finish operations centered at Polideportivo Llandrius, Camprodon. Long/medium routes start 08:00, short route 08:15.

history.origin_story: Debuted July 2, 2023 as a Camprodon-based sportive through Beget and Vallter, drawing 600+ riders on its first outing; part of the Gran Fondo 360 series alongside two other Catalan events. The organizer's official media archive documents further editions in 2024, 2025 and 2026, though the exact route has changed year to year (the 2024 long route used a temporary 130 km/2,400m course through Vallter, materially different from 2026's 144 km/3,600m).

biased_opinion.summary: Gran Fondo Pirineus is a real, multi-year Catalan sportive out of Camprodon — running since July 2023 with editions documented through 2026, three route lengths, and a genuine signature climb up Vallter 2000 on the long course. The organizer is upfront that it's noncompetitive: official regulations state roads stay open to traffic and no time lists are produced. Where the on-file profile fell short was accuracy, not honesty — the prior cutoff time ('4 hours, must finish by 4:00 PM') was simply wrong; the actual window is roughly 8 hours (08:00 start, 16:00 close) for the long and medium routes. Field-size reporting also conflicts across sources for the 2024 edition (600 per a Catalan government report vs. over 1,000 per a university outlet), so treat any specific number as unverified.
strengths: Genuine, established Pyrenean terrain — Coll de Capsacosta, Coll de Pera, Santigosa, Coll de Rocabruna, and the Vallter 2000 climb from Setcases (~12 km at 7.18%, up to 14%) on the long route
Multi-year operation since its July 2023 debut, with further editions documented in 2024, 2025 and 2026
Three route lengths (144/122/78 km) let riders self-select difficulty without losing the shared start/finish atmosphere
Organized support: aid stations, basic mechanical assistance, medical/evacuation coverage, and a following vehicle
The organizer is transparent about the noncompetitive format in its own regulations, rather than hiding it in fine print
weaknesses: Noncompetitive by the organizer's own regulations — roads stay open to traffic and no time lists are produced, so riders expecting a ranked result from the 'gran fondo' name and finish-time format should recalibrate
The route itself isn't fixed year to year: 2024 used a temporary 130 km/2,400m course, materially different from 2026's 144 km/3,600m — don't assume continuity of terrain across editions
No reliable, single field-size figure exists; a Catalan government report and a university outlet give conflicting 2024 counts (600 vs. over 1,000)
No 2027 date published yet as of this writing
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Go for genuine Pyrenean climbing on open roads with real organizational support and honest noncompetitive framing — just don't expect a timed, ranked result.

final_verdict.one_liner: Real Pyrenean climbs out of Camprodon, run as an openly noncompetitive supported sportive.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want genuine Pyrenean climbing (Vallter 2000 on the long route) with organized support and don't need a competitive result — the organizer explicitly runs it noncompetitive with no time lists.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a comparable Pyrenean/Catalan sportive with genuine competitive timing, look at other UCI- or federation-sanctioned Spanish gran fondos rather than assuming this one delivers ranked results.

videos.description:
Buena familia, 👋

este es el primer video de una carrera en la que he participado del CANAL! 

La hice hace un par de semanas en la zona norte de Cataluña pegando a los Pirineos, podréis ver que las vistas son increíbles y lo puertos duros.

Me lo pasé de maravilla y espero que a vosotros os guste también.

¿QUE PENSAIS? dejadlo en COMENTARIOS.

Si te ha gustado el video, deja tu LIKE y deja tu opinión en COMENTARIOS! 👍

No olvides SUSCRIBIRTE para no perderte nada de tu canal de ciclismo favorito!! No olvides activas las NOTIFICACIONES para no perderte el próximo video y, mientras tanto...

Instagram:  @call_of_cycling

¡¡Gracias por ver el video y bienvenido a la comunidad de CALL OF CYCLING!!💙🚴‍♂️

¡Un abrazo!

rider_intel.search_text: No verified rider intel currently exists for Gran Fondo Pirineus. (Two cross-race-mislabeled videos and their derived intel were purged 2026-07-24, editorial wave 5 — they documented an unrelated personal multi-day tour, not this organized event; see git history for detail.)

catalog_flags.status_note: fondo_rating.discipline is 'gran_fondo' but the organizer's own regulations explicitly describe this as a noncompetitive road sportive/marcha cicloturista — roads stay open to traffic and no time lists are produced, despite riders receiving a finish window. This doesn't cleanly fit a scoring taxonomy built around competitive gran fondos (field_depth, prestige and organization dimensions implicitly assume ranked competition). Recommend a human call on reclassifying discipline or adding scoring caveats, consistent with how flandrien-ride.json flags similar taxonomy mismatches rather than silently changing the discipline field.

eligibility.notes: Fourth consecutive edition ran 28 June 2026 in Camprodon (debuted July 2023); curl-verified 200 (2026-07-24). No 2027 date published yet as of this writing. Corrected this wave: distance_km 143.2->144 (organizer/federation both publish 144 km; 143.2 appears to be a GPX-track measurement, not the advertised distance); cutoff_time corrected from the clearly-wrong '4 hours (must finish by 4:00 PM)' to the actual ~8-hour window (08:00 start, 16:00 close) for the long/medium routes; field_size changed from the placeholder 'N/A' to null since 2024 attendance reporting conflicts by source (Generalitat de Catalunya reports 600 for 2024; Blanquerna reports over 1,000 for the same 2024 edition) and no reliable 2026 figure exists; registration/entry_fee populated from RockTheSport/official regs. Via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research, editorial wave 5.
